concept: Adapt / Adjust to Conditions / Modify for Fit
description: To adjust oneself or something to new conditions, making changes that improve fit with environment or circumstances. 'Hinolu' implies flexibility and responsiveness—not rigid resistance to change but intelligent adjustment that maintains function or wellbeing under altered conditions.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'hi', the light breath of flexibility—ready to move, not fixed. The high 'i' perceives what conditions require. Then 'no' adjusts with nasal shaping, form finding new configuration. Finally 'lu' flows into the adapted state, settled but not rigid.
grammatical_notes: Functions as intransitive or transitive verb: 'mia hinolu.' (I adapt.) 'mia norae hinolu.' (I adapt a method.) Related to 'helui' (change) but emphasizes purposeful adjustment rather than simple transformation.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Climate adaptation requires flexible response to changing conditions—communities that can adapt will survive and thrive.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Adaptation is wisdom in action—seeing conditions clearly and responding skillfully rather than insisting reality match our preferences.
